requirements.paraphrase: Give the model a structured task-input capability in the same conceptual manner as a tool.
Convert every invocation of the reserved capability into a runtime input_required interaction.
Keep the model-facing capability from reading standard input, accessing a terminal, rendering widgets, or owning persistence.
Describe semantic input types in each request.
Avoid prescribing a widget and preserve stable choices and values across semantically equivalent native presentations.
Resolve the pending model request and continue the same logical run after an answered resolution.
Keep the canonical lifecycle independent of provider tool-call syntax and treat plain prose questions only as degraded fallback.
Advertise the capability only when the host can resolve it while attached or persist both request and continuation for later resolution.
Govern availability by host interaction capability rather than ordinary domain-tool permissions.
Represent decline, cancellation, timeout, unavailability, and expiry explicitly.
Never manufacture a human answer when no human supplied one.
Keep clarification, approval, steering, and authentication as distinct interaction classes.
Request input only when it materially affects the result and cannot be recovered safely from context.
Inspect context, prefer one focused question, offer concrete consequential choices, and continue on a safe reversible assumption when possible.
Distinguish task input, action approval, steering, and authentication by origin, meaning, and resolution effect.
Never let a task-input answer grant action approval.
Never deliver an approval response as ordinary task content unless a separate policy exposes a safe summary.
Never collect authentication material through the normal task-input contract.
Make the interaction broker own identity, correlation, origin, capability negotiation, delivery, persistence, validation, idempotency, cancellation, timeout, expiry, and privacy-aware observability.
requirements.owner: interaction broker and model capability composition

requirements.paraphrase: Limit renderers to presentation ownership.
Prevent renderers from redefining request meaning or execution state.
Expose one reserved provider-neutral model capability meaning request task input from the user.
Bound typed questions, declare required or advisory intent, await a defined resolution, return it to the pending model request, preserve human provenance, and remain unavailable to incapable hosts.
Normally contain one question per request.
Allow a small host-rendered bundle for closely related decisions.
Limit the initial request contract to at most three questions.
Never execute a domain action, read process input directly, ask for secrets, approve an operation, assume a renderer, or label a timeout/default as human.
Account for input requests separately from domain tool execution.
Include opaque request identity, full execution correlation, typed stable question IDs, required/advisory intent, a reason, creation time, and lifecycle state.
Give each question a stable ID, concise prompt, semantic type, and accepted-response requirement.
Allow bounded headers, help, choices, recommendations, defaults, Other permission, validation constraints, and advisory presentation hints.
Key answers by stable question ID rather than displayed text.
Support confirmation, text, multiline text, single selection, and multiple selection while excluding nested forms, conditional subforms, uploads, and executable UI.
Give every choice a stable machine value and human-readable label.
Describe non-obvious choice consequences while allowing labels to change without changing stored or returned values.
Allow a tagged free-form Other alternative instead of a literal choice value.
Present structured choices first and open text entry only after the free-form alternative is selected.
Express compactness and native-control presentation preferences as hints.
Keep presentation hints advisory so hosts may choose semantically equivalent accessible controls.
Resolve a request exactly once as answered, declined, cancelled, timed_out, unavailable, expired, or superseded.
Identify the request, keyed answers, per-answer provenance, and resolution time in an answered resolution.
Distinguish human, explicit host/caller default, and policy provenance, and describe only explicit human input as human-selected or entered.
Validate response types, choice values, required fields, and current request state before continuation.
Stop consuming model tokens and executing dependent tools while a required request is unresolved, following the attached or detached continuation lifecycle.
Keep required input unresolved until an explicit terminal resolution category occurs.
Never silently select a required default because a timer elapsed.
Return an explicit non-answer outcome to the model when required input cannot be presented or resumed.
Apply a host-controlled waiting budget to advisory input.
Preserve an advisory budget expiry as timed_out provenance.
Mark a subsequently applied advisory default as default rather than human provenance.
Pause inactivity-based timeout accounting while the human is actively using the control.
Render confirmation, selections, text, multiline text, and related bundles through appropriate native terminal controls.
Provide navigation, shortcuts, visible recommendations, Other entry, distinct cancel/decline actions, and accessible text fallbacks.
Never collect later clarification from an exhausted piped initial-input stream.
Use a controlling terminal as a separate interactive control channel while keeping prompt and control input separate.
Return machine-readable input_required or explicit unavailable when no interactive control channel exists.
Never hang reading an input stream that cannot produce an answer.
Keep human interaction UI out of answer-only output, structured model output, and machine event streams.
Use the terminal presentation channel for interactive controls.
Emit a structured request event with stable correlation for machine-oriented CLI control.
Never imply detached resumability for a sessionless run after its continuation is discarded.
Distinguish input-control cancellation, decline, containing-run cancellation, and unsolicited steering.
Make keyboard interruption behavior predictable and explicit before destructive run cancellation.
Allow embedded hosts to register a complete semantic async handler that resolves, waits, detaches, and receives cancellation.
Permit an SDK handler to remain pending without blocking unrelated application work.
Give detached hosts sufficient opaque correlation without provider-specific reconstruction.
Allow applications to reject or decline requests they cannot safely render.
Require an explicit headless policy covering predeclared values, controllers, persistence, defaults with non-human provenance, or unavailability.
Never let headless execution block indefinitely, consume arbitrary stdin, silently answer required input, or turn clarification into approval.
Emit machine-readable pauses that external orchestrators can display and resolve with correlation.
Publish server interaction lifecycle events separately from answer text and model reasoning, including create, delivery, resolution, terminal outcomes, and resume.
Authenticate, authorize, correlate, and idempotently process remote resolution submission.
Use a documented Avalan interaction envelope on transports without native user input.
Accompany structured requests with readable text for clients unable to render forms.
Project ordinary Avalan task input to negotiated MCP elicitation.
Preserve negotiated semantic types and accept, decline, cancel separation while keeping authentication outside form elicitation and returning data to the origin.
Project durable suspended execution as an MCP task in input_required when task semantics are available.
Use an explicitly supported structured fallback or resolve unavailable when the MCP client lacks required capability.
Never wait indefinitely for an elicitation response the MCP client cannot send.
Route downstream MCP-server elicitation through the same broker while preserving downstream origin.
Project required Avalan input as an A2A task in INPUT_REQUIRED state.
Preserve task, context, request identity, readable content, structured validation data, and same-task follow-up correlation.
Carry rich form semantics in a negotiated extension or structured data part with readable fallback.
Never use ambiguous plain text to resolve an arbitrary pending request.
Preserve the originating agent and execution branch on every request.
Allow nested agents to request input only when the containing orchestration and host can route and resume it.
Surface safe origin context, route only to the origin branch, serialize confusing prompts, queue independently, and cancel child requests with parent cancellation.
Permit at most one unresolved required request per execution branch.
Detect or bound materially equivalent repeated requests to prevent clarification loops.
Scope each request to the trusted user, tenant, session, task, turn, and execution branch and apply first-valid-resolution-wins.
Treat an authorized semantically identical resolution as idempotent.
Prevent a conflicting later resolution from resuming execution again.
Reject responses to expired, cancelled, or superseded requests as stale.
Reject resolutions from unauthorized principals.
Expose only opaque continuation references outside the runtime.
Never interpret client disconnection as decline; apply the declared pending, unavailable, timeout, or cancellation policy.
Never treat clarification text such as yes or continue as protected-action authorization without a separate approval resolution.
Never request passwords, keys, tokens, private keys, financial credentials, or authentication challenges through normal task input.
Treat model-authored prompts and choice descriptions as untrusted presentation data.
Never execute markup, links, terminal escapes, or embedded instructions from model-authored presentation data.
Expose content-safe telemetry for lifecycle, wait time, category, surface, validation, duplicates, staleness, cancellation, timeout, expiry, and resume.
Preserve correlation in telemetry without logging raw answers by default.
Apply conversation-data retention, access, redaction, and deletion policy to prompts, choices, answers, and multiline content.
Redact accidentally entered secret-like values from logs and diagnostics without treating the channel as credential-safe.
Keep human, default, and policy provenance distinct in traces and audit records.
Define deterministic behavior for all fifteen frozen failure conditions across every applicable public surface.
Prevent interactive-presentation failures from corrupting transcript, answer stream, or persisted execution state.
Prove the complete public attached cli clarification behavior through a supported product boundary.
Prove the complete public text and multiple selection behavior through a supported product boundary.
Prove the complete public piped prompt with tty behavior through a supported product boundary.
Prove the complete public fully headless run behavior through a supported product boundary.
Prove the complete public required versus advisory behavior through a supported product boundary.
Prove the complete public decline cancel and steering behavior through a supported product boundary.
Prove the complete public approval isolation behavior through a supported product boundary.
Prove the complete public mcp projection behavior through a supported product boundary.
Prove the complete public a2a projection behavior through a supported product boundary.
Prove the complete public multi agent origin behavior through a supported product boundary.
Prove the complete public idempotency and staleness behavior through a supported product boundary.
Prove the complete public privacy behavior through a supported product boundary.
Freeze complete behavioral and public-scenario traceability.
Enforce planned versus active acceptance lifecycle and append-only activation.
Freeze and exhaustively verify every failure-condition and surface cell.
Collect and execute exact acceptance nodes while rejecting non-evidence outcomes.
Enforce exact total, per-file, source-inventory, evidence-line, and exclusion-baseline coverage.
Provide fail-fast exact Make and database-backed quality targets with CI metadata locks.
Track and execute lifecycle-aware positive and negative static type contracts.
Provide typed deterministic clocks, IDs, providers, barriers, principals, correlations, and local peers.
Freeze every identity, state, outcome, capability, protocol, privacy, error, bound, replay, and activation decision.
Inventory every obsolete coupling, result shape, correlation, mutable field, and assertion for atomic removal.
Record passing baseline and implementation evidence with exact commands and counts.
Keep canonical production types dormant and unadvertised until the atomic capability activation boundary.
